raw_inputs.friction_analysis_prompt:
TASK: Cognitive Friction Analysis (Table Output Only)

Role: Principal Enterprise AI Architect and Business Transformation Strategist.
Mode: High reasoning.

Goal
Identify "Cognitive Friction" in the provided process documents: places where humans act as middleware (e.g., transcribing data, making routine decisions, bridging system gaps).

Inputs
You will receive process maps, process documentation, regional nuances, and benchmark/best-practice material.

Output (STRICT)
Produce ONLY a table titled "Cognitive Friction Analysis".
Do not include narrative explanation outside the table.

Rules
- Use ONLY what is explicitly supported by the provided documents.
- If a detail is not stated, write "Not specified" and add it as an open question.
- Each row represents ONE friction point (one manual bottleneck).
- Provide evidence per row: document name + page/section + short quote/paraphrase.

Table Columns (STRICT)
- Friction_ID (F-001, F-002…)
- Current_Manual_Action (what humans do)
- Where_in_Process (step/activity name as stated)
- Trigger_or_Input_Channel (only if stated)
- Region_Impacted (Global or specific regions only if stated)
- Systems_or_Tools_Mentioned (only what is stated; else "Not specified")
- Why_It's_Friction (1–2 lines: delay, rework, errors, compliance risk, etc.)
- Evidence (Document + page/section + quote/paraphrase)
- Open_Questions (only when needed)

Begin
Read the inputs and output the "Cognitive Friction Analysis" table only.

Task
NORTH AMERICA (NA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Esker, CSV (OpenText), EDI
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (exceptions, new SKUs, data sync
failures)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order with order number generated in SAP
Process Note:
• "EDI & Esker are dominant for order intake (Canada & US domestic orders)"
• "OpenText is heavily used for automated entry (Export orders -- JKSEAT region)"
• "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data sync
failures"
• "For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/ship to and
different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with a full
container load"
• "CSV file transfers are unique to NA, used by Canada domestic for some samples and by
JKSEAT region for customer orders"
Key Comment: "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data
sync failures. For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/
ship to and different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with
a full container load."
Unique Aspect: CSV file processing unique to North America; OpenText boundary system
for export orders; Frequent manual review due to diversity of intake channels and customer
changes
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 usage already established; Need to ensure Esker and
OpenText integration validated in S/4HANA; CSV file handling process must be maintained
 
MEXICO
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service, QSR, Retail), SAP VA01 (Assumption), Edicom/EDI
(Retail), Gmail (Food service)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service via Thalamus) / Automated (EDI for retail)
McCain    5|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System order generated by platform (Thalamus/EDI/SAP)
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is the main platform for food service and QSR; Edicom EDI is used for retail"
• "Manual entry is common, especially for food service; EDI is automated for retail"
• "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer communication"
• "Order acknowledgment can be automated or manual, and may be requested by the
customer"
• "Common check points: price, route, warehouse, weight, duplicate order, requested delivery
date, etc."
Key Comment: "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer
communication"
Unique Aspect: Gmail extensively used for food service operational communications;
Thalamus serves multiple channels (Food service, QSR, Retail); Edicom EDI specific to retail
channel
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus and Edicom EDI integrations must be validated;
Gmail-based workflow formalization needed; VA01 standard transaction confirmed
 
CALATIN (Central America & Caribbean)
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI (Retail), Day & Ross (DRTN
for NA-sourced)
Manual/Automated: Manual (frequent) / Automated (EDI for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record in SAP
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; SAP for system entry"
• "Manual entry is frequent, especially for exports and smaller segments"
• "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks"
• "NA-sourced orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with full traceability for export
compliance"
Key Comment: "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks" and "NA-sourced
orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
Unique Aspect: Export-focused operations with enhanced documentation requirements; Day
& Ross (DRTN) integration for NA-sourced orders; Thalamus for food service segment
SAP Migration Consideration: Export documentation workflows must be preserved; DRTN
integration for NA-sourced orders needs validation; Manual export handling processes require
clear procedures in S/4HANA
 
ANDEAN (Colombia)
System/Tool: Thalamus, Carvajal, SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI
Manual/Automated: Manual (portal handling) / Automated (EDI/Carvajal for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "Thalamus and Carvajal are primary intake platforms; SAP is used for system entry"
McCain    6|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Manual portal handling, especially for food service (Actualmente se esta migrando a
Thalamus)"
• "EDI/Carvajal standards apply for retail and exports"
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with data retention for
compliance"
Key Comment: "Manual portal handling, especially for food service.(Actualmente se esta
migrando a Thalamus)"
Unique Aspect: Carvajal platform specific to Andean region for retail; Migration to Thalamus
in progress; EDI/Carvajal standards for retail and exports
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus migration ongoing - ensure smooth transition;
Carvajal integration must be validated in S/4HANA; Data retention for compliance mandatory
 
BRAZIL
System/Tool: SAP Fiori (Primary), SAP ECC VA01 (Exceptions: CPU, McD, BK, maritime,
donations/samples), EDI, Neo Grid (Retail)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fiori entries) / Automated (EDI, Neo Grid)
Performer: CSR (Fiori) / SALES ADM Team (SAP ECC exceptions)
Output: SAP order generated in Fiori or ECC depending on scenario
Process Note:
• "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used for special
cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)"
• "SALES ADM team manages SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
• "WhatsApp/email are common for customer communication and error resolution"
• "Proforma invoices for CPU orders are issued via SAP ECC VF01/VF02"
• "Strong SAP Fiori adoption; exceptions handled manually in ECC"
Key Comment: "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used
for special cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)" and "SALES ADM team manages
SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
Unique Aspect: Dual system architecture (Fiori primary + ECC exceptions); SALES ADM team
handles special scenarios; Strong Fiori adoption among all LATAM regions; WhatsApp business
communication; Proforma invoice generation (VF01/VF02)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual system (Fiori + ECC) needs consolidation
strategy in S/4HANA; Special handling for CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples must be
accommodated; SALES ADM team workflows require clear definition; Proforma invoice process
(VF01/VF02) validation
 
URUGUAY
System/Tool: Thalamus, Power Street, EDI, SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (domestic via Thalamus/Power Street) / Automated (EDI retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "EDI, Thalamus, and Power Street are used for order intake; Power Street is unique to
Uruguay"
• "Manual entry is common for domestic orders; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and email are standard for customer communication and error resolution"
McCain    7|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Power Street: Tool to input customer orders for direct and indirect sales. This is used to
load orders on every truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest
of McCain. Orders are loaded on the spot (trucks)."
Key Comment: "Power Street is unique to Uruguay" and "This is used to load orders on every
truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest of McCain. Orders are
loaded on the spot (trucks)."
Unique Aspect: Power Street ONLY exists in Uruguay; On-the-spot truck loading model;
Unique sales operational model different from rest of McCain; Direct and indirect sales via
mobile tool
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Power Street unique to Uruguay must be integrated
or replaced in S/4HANA; Mobile truck loading workflow is business-critical; Operational model
significantly different from other regions
 
ARGENTINA
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), Krikos360/EDI (Retail), Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone (QSR),
SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service, QSR) / Automated (EDI retail via Krikos360)
Performer: CSR / Operations (varies by channel)
Output: System order by platform (Thalamus/Krikos360/SAP)
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; Krikos360/EDI for retail; Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone for
QSR"
• "Manual entry is standard for food service and QSR; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication"
• "Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy; EDI for retail"
Key Comment: "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication" and
"Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy"
Unique Aspect: Krikos360 platform (Planexware) specific to Argentina retail; Multi-channel
digital communication (Gmail, WhatsApp, Phone) for QSR; Channel-specific tool strategy
SAP Migration Consideration: Krikos360/Planexware integration validation critical; WhatsApp/
Gmail workflow formalization needed; Multi-channel tool landscape requires consolidation
assessment
 
APAC-ANZ (Australia & New Zealand)
System/Tool: Portalink, EDI, Email, SAP VA02/VA03 (NOT VA01 for EDI)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Portalink) / Manual (email-based changes)
Performer: Customer Solutions Team
Output: SAP sales order
Process Note:
• "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders, regardless of
how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on email"
• "Portalink Integration: Serves as critical boundary system converting non-EDI orders into
SAP-compatible format, enabling immediate automated acknowledgements for non-EDI
customers"
• "ANZ: VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
• "HighRadius Financial Integration: Used for credit checks and payment tracking"
• "Excel Macro Automation: End-of-day order confirmations automated through Excel
macros"
McCain    8|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Z7 CSR Hold Implementation: Universal application of Z7 CSR hold enables manual review
and allocation rule enforcement by commercial team"
Key Comment: "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders,
regardless of how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on
email" and "VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
Unique Aspect: CRITICAL: VA01 NOT used for EDI order entry - ANZ uses VA02/VA03;
Portalink boundary system converts non-EDI to SAP format; HighRadius for credit/payment;
Excel macro for end-of-day confirmations; Z7 CSR Hold universal; Duplicate EDI order manual
deletion required
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ANZ does NOT use VA01 for EDI orders (uses VA02/
VA03) - This is UNIQUE and must be carefully evaluated in S/4HANA migration; Portalink
boundary system integration critical; HighRadius integration validation; Excel macro process
may need modernization; Z7 CSR Hold implementation; ATP functionality gap (currently no ATP)
 
APAC-CHINA
System/Tool: Digital Hub (Cloud-based portal - MANDATORY for ALL orders), SAP VA01 (via
EDI integration from Digital Hub)
Manual/Automated: Manual entry into Digital Hub / Automated (Digital Hub to SAP via EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order triggered via EDI from Digital Hub after validation
Process Note:
• "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and processed
through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal"
• "Orders are manually entered into the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are
performed before triggering sales orders into SAP via EDI integration"
• "No Direct Customer EDI: All orders flow through Digital Hub regardless of customer
technical capability"
• "Real-time Integration: Direct integration with warehouse management systems provides
accurate, current inventory data for ATP decisions"
• "Auto-cancellation Logic: Sophisticated cancellation workflows with reason codes (Y1, Y5) for
unpaid orders enable automated revenue protection"
• "ZF delivery blocks preferred over credit blocks for payment terms enforcement"
Key Comment: "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and
processed through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal" and "Orders are manually entered into
the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are performed before triggering sales orders
into SAP via EDI integration"
Unique Aspect: Digital Hub MANDATORY for 100% of orders regardless of channel; No
direct customer-to-SAP EDI; Dual ATP (Digital Hub "light" + SAP "final"); ZF delivery block for
prepayment instead of credit blocks; Auto-cancel Y5 rejection code for unpaid prepay orders;
WMS real-time integration; Channel-specific CRM (Food Service) vs email (Retail/QSR)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Digital Hub is mandatory gateway (no direct SAP
access); Digital Hub to S/4HANA EDI integration must be validated; Dual ATP mechanism must
be maintained; ZF delivery block logic; Auto-cancellation workflows (Y1, Y5); WMS integration
validation; Prepayment enforcement logic
 
APAC-INDIA
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI via OpenText (Reliance ONLY), Email, Spreadsheet,
Bheem DMS
McCain    9|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI for select modern trade) / Manual (DC staff entry for non-
EDI)
Performer: DC Executives / Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order
Process Note:
• "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are automatically
integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or spreadsheet are
manually entered by distribution center staff"
• "DC-based Processing: Decentralized order entry model contrasts with other regions'
centralized approaches"
• "Minimal Automation: Limited automated order acknowledgements (only Reliance) reflects
low digitalization maturity"
• "Reliance Special Treatment: Only customer receiving automated order acknowledgements,
indicating strategic partnership requirements"
• "Manual-first Philosophy: Preference for manual processes over automation, possibly due
to complexity and control requirements"
• "MB5T used for stock in transit checks, crucial for multi-location inventory management"
Key Comment: "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are
automatically integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or
spreadsheet are manually entered by distribution center staff" and "Only customer receiving
automated order acknowledgements (Reliance)"
Unique Aspect: Decentralized DC-based order entry model; ONLY Reliance has EDI and
automated acknowledgement; Manual-first philosophy; OpenText for select modern
trade EDI; Export orders processed in monthly cycles; MB5T for stock in transit checks
(multi-location); Bheem DMS for retail claims; 100% credit block by default; FSSAI license
validation at order creation; TDP days (10/20/30) affect transport capacity
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Decentralized DC-based entry model unique to India;
OpenText integration for Reliance EDI; Manual entry by DC staff workflows; Minimal automation
- high manual dependency; MB5T stock in transit functionality; FSSAI license validation must be
maintained; Default credit block process; TDP days operational logic
 
APAC-JKSEAT (Japan, Korea, SE Asia & Taiwan)
System/Tool:
• Japan: SAP VA01/ME21 (Assumption), Fax, Customer websites, EDI, CSV macro
• KSEAT: SAP VA01 (Assumption), CSV files, Macro/EDI watcher, Email
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fax, websites in Japan) / Automated (CSV macro, EDI for both)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order, Proforma invoice (as order confirmation)
Process Note:
• Japan: "Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer websites,
EDI, and CSV files. Fax and website orders are manually entered into SAP , while EDI and CSV
file orders are automatically integrated into SAP"
• KSEAT: "Orders are received via email as either CSV files or manual requests. CSV files are
uploaded to a shared folder, then picked up by EDI or a macro for automatic entry into SAP ,
while manual orders are entered directly into SAP"
• "Traditional Communication Channels: Japan maintains fax communication alongside
modern channels, reflecting local business culture and customer preferences"
McCain    10|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Advanced Order Planning: Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance eliminates need
for real-time inventory visibility but requires sophisticated planning"
• "CSV Automation: KSEAT uses shared folder mechanism with macro/EDI pickup for CSV
automation"
• "Proforma Invoice as confirmation: KC sends Proforma Invoice (manual PDF from SAP) as
order confirmation; HAVI/McDonald's excluded"
• "No local ATP/MOQ gating at JKC; orders are taken weeks to months ahead (min ~6 weeks),
and sourcing regions (e.g., NA, EU) run ATP on their side"
Key Comment: "Japan: Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer
websites, EDI, and CSV files" and "Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance" and "No local
ATP/MOQ gating at JKC"
Unique Aspect: Traditional fax still used in Japan; CSV macro/shared folder automation
in KSEAT; Proforma invoice used as order confirmation (not standard acknowledgement);
No local ATP - sourcing regions handle ATP; Long lead times (6 weeks to 4 months); ME21 for
purchase orders alongside VA01; Power BI for shipment tracking; Regional orders 6-16 weeks
ahead of ship date
SAP Migration Consideration: Japan VA01/ME21 usage must be validated; Fax integration/
workflow documentation needed; CSV macro automation from shared folders; Proforma invoice
generation process; No local ATP - sourcing region dependency; Long planning horizon (6
weeks-4 months) must be supported; Power BI integration for tracking
 
GREAT BRITAIN (GB)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption for order creation), EDI, Esker, Email (Manual), ZV05N
(for display)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (email orders)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: Manually entered order (from email) / Automated order from EDI/Esker
Process Note:
• "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email (manual entry
required), and Esker (PDF document processing)"
• "Transaction codes used: VA01 for order creation, ZV05N for displaying orders, VA02 for
order amendments"
• "EDI orders undergo only basic sense checks; no comprehensive price validation at entry"
• "if there is an error in the EDI it will generate a failed idoc which can be viewed in WE05"
• "Incompletion log in SAP flags missing data, causing EDI orders to fail and go on hold,
equally if the PO is a duplicate SAP puts the order on hold"
• "Manual orders similarly have no price check at entry; incompletion log identifies gaps"
• "Manual process only - no automatic customer notification upon order entry"
• "All orders entered using t-code VA01, where the order type is specified"
Key Comment: "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email
(manual entry required), and Esker (PDF document processing)" and "Manual process only - no
automatic customer notification upon order entry" and "All orders entered using t-code VA01,
where the order type is specified"
Unique Aspect: ZV05N used for displaying orders (alongside VA03); WE05 for failed IDoc
monitoring; Esker reads PDF documents from customers; No automatic acknowledgement
- manual email confirmation only; No price validation at entry - handled later as claims via
AR inbox; Incompletion log flags missing data; Duplicate PO auto-hold; EDI MDN available on
customer request
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 standard usage confirmed; ZV05N display transaction
validation in S/4HANA; WE05 IDoc monitoring; Esker PDF integration; Manual acknowledgement
McCain    11|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
process; No price validation at entry - pricing claims handled downstream; Incompletion log
dependency
 
EMENA (Europe, Middle East, North Africa)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), ZVA05N (import transaction), EDI, Esker, CSV process
(Italy), Manual (email/phone)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker, CSV Italy) / Manual (email/phone)
Performer: Customer Service Representative / System (for off-time import)
Output: Orders imported into SAP
Process Note:
• "Order requests could be received via various platforms: Manual entry (email & phone call),
ESKER (reads PDF documents from customers), EDI, CSV process (Italy) via EDI"
• "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
• "Only applicable to EMENA"
• T-codes mentioned: "VA01, VA02, ZVA05N, ZG_MTD_SHIPMENT, VL01N, VL02N, VL06"
Key Comment: "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
and "Only applicable to EMENA"
Unique Aspect: ZVA05N import transaction ONLY in EMENA; Off-time import process
unique to this region; CSV process specific to Italy via EDI; Esker PDF document processing;
Multi-channel intake (EDI, Esker, CSV, Manual); Import batch processing during off-peak hours
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ZVA05N off-time import transaction unique to
EMENA must be evaluated for S/4HANA equivalent; Italy CSV process via EDI; Esker integration;
Batch import scheduling and timing; Multi-channel consolidation during off-time import
 
SOUTH AFRICA (SA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Email (Direct), EDI via Vector (Indirect 3PL)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Direct Network) / Automated (Indirect Network via Vector EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Representative (Direct) / Vector System (Indirect)
Output: Manually entered order (Direct) / EDI order via Vector (Indirect)
Process Note:
• "Purchase requests received via email" (Direct Network)
• "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network"
• "Indirect Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from
customers; next day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP
(boundary system), operating on a consignment model"
• "(Direct) Customer Service receives purchase requests via email or manual channels and
manually enters into SAP . T-code VA01 used for order creation"
• "(Indirect) EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers"
• "Proforma invoice/manual order confirmation for some customers"
Key Comment: "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network" and "Indirect
Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers; next
day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP (boundary system),
operating on a consignment model"
Unique Aspect: Dual network model - Direct vs Indirect (3PL/Vector); Vector 3PL ONLY in
South Africa; Consignment model for indirect network; Next-day backward integration to
SAP; Proforma invoice for some customers; T-code VA01 confirmed for order creation; Manual
entry for direct network
McCain    12|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual network model (Direct manual vs Indirect
Vector EDI); Vector 3PL boundary system integration critical; Consignment model logic; Next-day
backward integration timing and process; Proforma invoice generation for specific customers;
VA01 standard usage
(as-is) IT System: SAP , Digital Hub, Excel(CSV), Thalamus
IT Systems:
- SAP ECC
SAP/SSC/Executable: SAP - VA01, VA02
Organization units:  01.05.01.03 Enter Order Into System - Customer Solutions/3PL
Dictionary entries: SAP ECC
Task type: Receive
Enter Consignment Details into the ERP
Task
NORTH AMERICA (NA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Esker, CSV (OpenText), EDI
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (exceptions, new SKUs, data sync
failures)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order with order number generated in SAP
Process Note:
• "EDI & Esker are dominant for order intake (Canada & US domestic orders)"
• "OpenText is heavily used for automated entry (Export orders -- JKSEAT region)"
• "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data sync
failures"
• "For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/ship to and
different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with a full
container load"
• "CSV file transfers are unique to NA, used by Canada domestic for some samples and by
JKSEAT region for customer orders"
Key Comment: "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data
sync failures. For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/
ship to and different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with
a full container load."
Unique Aspect: CSV file processing unique to North America; OpenText boundary system
for export orders; Frequent manual review due to diversity of intake channels and customer
changes
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 usage already established; Need to ensure Esker and
OpenText integration validated in S/4HANA; CSV file handling process must be maintained
 
MEXICO
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service, QSR, Retail), SAP VA01 (Assumption), Edicom/EDI
(Retail), Gmail (Food service)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service via Thalamus) / Automated (EDI for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System order generated by platform (Thalamus/EDI/SAP)
Process Note:
McCain    13|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Thalamus is the main platform for food service and QSR; Edicom EDI is used for retail"
• "Manual entry is common, especially for food service; EDI is automated for retail"
• "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer communication"
• "Order acknowledgment can be automated or manual, and may be requested by the
customer"

SAP ECC (Enterprise Central Component)
SAP ECC (ERP Central Component) is a comprehensive enterprise resource planning (ERP)
software suite developed by SAP . It serves as the core component of many organizations’ IT
landscapes, integrating key business processes and data across departments, and enabling
efficient management of resources, operations, and reporting.Key 
Features of SAP ECC:Centralized database for seamless data flow across business functions
• Modular structure, allowing organizations to implement only the modules they need
• Real-time processing and reporting capabilities
• Strong integration with other SAP and third-party systems
SAP CO, a module within SAP ECC that focuses on internal accounting and management
accounting, providing tools for planning, controlling, and analyzing costs and revenues. It is tightly
integrated with SAP FI (Financial Accounting) and is often referred to as SAP FICO. SAP CO helps
businesses track costs associated with various business processes, allocate costs, and manage
profitability analysis.

1.2 Description
This process describes the activities to input validated order information into the order management
system for further processing
McCain    4|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
1.3 Elements
Process elements
01.05.01.01 Receive Customer Order Request
Start Message Event

Task
NORTH AMERICA (NA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Esker, CSV (OpenText), EDI
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (exceptions, new SKUs, data sync
failures)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order with order number generated in SAP
Process Note:
• "EDI & Esker are dominant for order intake (Canada & US domestic orders)"
• "OpenText is heavily used for automated entry (Export orders -- JKSEAT region)"
• "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data sync
failures"
• "For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/ship to and
different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with a full
container load"
• "CSV file transfers are unique to NA, used by Canada domestic for some samples and by
JKSEAT region for customer orders"
Key Comment: "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data
sync failures. For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/
ship to and different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with
a full container load."
Unique Aspect: CSV file processing unique to North America; OpenText boundary system
for export orders; Frequent manual review due to diversity of intake channels and customer
changes
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 usage already established; Need to ensure Esker and
OpenText integration validated in S/4HANA; CSV file handling process must be maintained
 
MEXICO
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service, QSR, Retail), SAP VA01 (Assumption), Edicom/EDI
(Retail), Gmail (Food service)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service via Thalamus) / Automated (EDI for retail)
McCain    5|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System order generated by platform (Thalamus/EDI/SAP)
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is the main platform for food service and QSR; Edicom EDI is used for retail"
• "Manual entry is common, especially for food service; EDI is automated for retail"
• "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer communication"
• "Order acknowledgment can be automated or manual, and may be requested by the
customer"
• "Common check points: price, route, warehouse, weight, duplicate order, requested delivery
date, etc."
Key Comment: "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer
communication"
Unique Aspect: Gmail extensively used for food service operational communications;
Thalamus serves multiple channels (Food service, QSR, Retail); Edicom EDI specific to retail
channel
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus and Edicom EDI integrations must be validated;
Gmail-based workflow formalization needed; VA01 standard transaction confirmed
 
CALATIN (Central America & Caribbean)
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI (Retail), Day & Ross (DRTN
for NA-sourced)
Manual/Automated: Manual (frequent) / Automated (EDI for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record in SAP
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; SAP for system entry"
• "Manual entry is frequent, especially for exports and smaller segments"
• "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks"
• "NA-sourced orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with full traceability for export
compliance"
Key Comment: "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks" and "NA-sourced
orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
Unique Aspect: Export-focused operations with enhanced documentation requirements; Day
& Ross (DRTN) integration for NA-sourced orders; Thalamus for food service segment
SAP Migration Consideration: Export documentation workflows must be preserved; DRTN
integration for NA-sourced orders needs validation; Manual export handling processes require
clear procedures in S/4HANA
 
ANDEAN (Colombia)
System/Tool: Thalamus, Carvajal, SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI
Manual/Automated: Manual (portal handling) / Automated (EDI/Carvajal for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "Thalamus and Carvajal are primary intake platforms; SAP is used for system entry"
McCain    6|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Manual portal handling, especially for food service (Actualmente se esta migrando a
Thalamus)"
• "EDI/Carvajal standards apply for retail and exports"
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with data retention for
compliance"
Key Comment: "Manual portal handling, especially for food service.(Actualmente se esta
migrando a Thalamus)"
Unique Aspect: Carvajal platform specific to Andean region for retail; Migration to Thalamus
in progress; EDI/Carvajal standards for retail and exports
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus migration ongoing - ensure smooth transition;
Carvajal integration must be validated in S/4HANA; Data retention for compliance mandatory
 
BRAZIL
System/Tool: SAP Fiori (Primary), SAP ECC VA01 (Exceptions: CPU, McD, BK, maritime,
donations/samples), EDI, Neo Grid (Retail)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fiori entries) / Automated (EDI, Neo Grid)
Performer: CSR (Fiori) / SALES ADM Team (SAP ECC exceptions)
Output: SAP order generated in Fiori or ECC depending on scenario
Process Note:
• "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used for special
cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)"
• "SALES ADM team manages SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
• "WhatsApp/email are common for customer communication and error resolution"
• "Proforma invoices for CPU orders are issued via SAP ECC VF01/VF02"
• "Strong SAP Fiori adoption; exceptions handled manually in ECC"
Key Comment: "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used
for special cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)" and "SALES ADM team manages
SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
Unique Aspect: Dual system architecture (Fiori primary + ECC exceptions); SALES ADM team
handles special scenarios; Strong Fiori adoption among all LATAM regions; WhatsApp business
communication; Proforma invoice generation (VF01/VF02)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual system (Fiori + ECC) needs consolidation
strategy in S/4HANA; Special handling for CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples must be
accommodated; SALES ADM team workflows require clear definition; Proforma invoice process
(VF01/VF02) validation
 
URUGUAY
System/Tool: Thalamus, Power Street, EDI, SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (domestic via Thalamus/Power Street) / Automated (EDI retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "EDI, Thalamus, and Power Street are used for order intake; Power Street is unique to
Uruguay"
• "Manual entry is common for domestic orders; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and email are standard for customer communication and error resolution"
McCain    7|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Power Street: Tool to input customer orders for direct and indirect sales. This is used to
load orders on every truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest
of McCain. Orders are loaded on the spot (trucks)."
Key Comment: "Power Street is unique to Uruguay" and "This is used to load orders on every
truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest of McCain. Orders are
loaded on the spot (trucks)."
Unique Aspect: Power Street ONLY exists in Uruguay; On-the-spot truck loading model;
Unique sales operational model different from rest of McCain; Direct and indirect sales via
mobile tool
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Power Street unique to Uruguay must be integrated
or replaced in S/4HANA; Mobile truck loading workflow is business-critical; Operational model
significantly different from other regions
 
ARGENTINA
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), Krikos360/EDI (Retail), Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone (QSR),
SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service, QSR) / Automated (EDI retail via Krikos360)
Performer: CSR / Operations (varies by channel)
Output: System order by platform (Thalamus/Krikos360/SAP)
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; Krikos360/EDI for retail; Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone for
QSR"
• "Manual entry is standard for food service and QSR; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication"
• "Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy; EDI for retail"
Key Comment: "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication" and
"Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy"
Unique Aspect: Krikos360 platform (Planexware) specific to Argentina retail; Multi-channel
digital communication (Gmail, WhatsApp, Phone) for QSR; Channel-specific tool strategy
SAP Migration Consideration: Krikos360/Planexware integration validation critical; WhatsApp/
Gmail workflow formalization needed; Multi-channel tool landscape requires consolidation
assessment
 
APAC-ANZ (Australia & New Zealand)
System/Tool: Portalink, EDI, Email, SAP VA02/VA03 (NOT VA01 for EDI)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Portalink) / Manual (email-based changes)
Performer: Customer Solutions Team
Output: SAP sales order
Process Note:
• "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders, regardless of
how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on email"
• "Portalink Integration: Serves as critical boundary system converting non-EDI orders into
SAP-compatible format, enabling immediate automated acknowledgements for non-EDI
customers"
• "ANZ: VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
• "HighRadius Financial Integration: Used for credit checks and payment tracking"
• "Excel Macro Automation: End-of-day order confirmations automated through Excel
macros"
McCain    8|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Z7 CSR Hold Implementation: Universal application of Z7 CSR hold enables manual review
and allocation rule enforcement by commercial team"
Key Comment: "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders,
regardless of how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on
email" and "VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
Unique Aspect: CRITICAL: VA01 NOT used for EDI order entry - ANZ uses VA02/VA03;
Portalink boundary system converts non-EDI to SAP format; HighRadius for credit/payment;
Excel macro for end-of-day confirmations; Z7 CSR Hold universal; Duplicate EDI order manual
deletion required
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ANZ does NOT use VA01 for EDI orders (uses VA02/
VA03) - This is UNIQUE and must be carefully evaluated in S/4HANA migration; Portalink
boundary system integration critical; HighRadius integration validation; Excel macro process
may need modernization; Z7 CSR Hold implementation; ATP functionality gap (currently no ATP)
 
APAC-CHINA
System/Tool: Digital Hub (Cloud-based portal - MANDATORY for ALL orders), SAP VA01 (via
EDI integration from Digital Hub)
Manual/Automated: Manual entry into Digital Hub / Automated (Digital Hub to SAP via EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order triggered via EDI from Digital Hub after validation
Process Note:
• "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and processed
through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal"
• "Orders are manually entered into the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are
performed before triggering sales orders into SAP via EDI integration"
• "No Direct Customer EDI: All orders flow through Digital Hub regardless of customer
technical capability"
• "Real-time Integration: Direct integration with warehouse management systems provides
accurate, current inventory data for ATP decisions"
• "Auto-cancellation Logic: Sophisticated cancellation workflows with reason codes (Y1, Y5) for
unpaid orders enable automated revenue protection"
• "ZF delivery blocks preferred over credit blocks for payment terms enforcement"
Key Comment: "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and
processed through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal" and "Orders are manually entered into
the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are performed before triggering sales orders
into SAP via EDI integration"
Unique Aspect: Digital Hub MANDATORY for 100% of orders regardless of channel; No
direct customer-to-SAP EDI; Dual ATP (Digital Hub "light" + SAP "final"); ZF delivery block for
prepayment instead of credit blocks; Auto-cancel Y5 rejection code for unpaid prepay orders;
WMS real-time integration; Channel-specific CRM (Food Service) vs email (Retail/QSR)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Digital Hub is mandatory gateway (no direct SAP
access); Digital Hub to S/4HANA EDI integration must be validated; Dual ATP mechanism must
be maintained; ZF delivery block logic; Auto-cancellation workflows (Y1, Y5); WMS integration
validation; Prepayment enforcement logic
 
APAC-INDIA
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI via OpenText (Reliance ONLY), Email, Spreadsheet,
Bheem DMS
McCain    9|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI for select modern trade) / Manual (DC staff entry for non-
EDI)
Performer: DC Executives / Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order
Process Note:
• "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are automatically
integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or spreadsheet are
manually entered by distribution center staff"
• "DC-based Processing: Decentralized order entry model contrasts with other regions'
centralized approaches"
• "Minimal Automation: Limited automated order acknowledgements (only Reliance) reflects
low digitalization maturity"
• "Reliance Special Treatment: Only customer receiving automated order acknowledgements,
indicating strategic partnership requirements"
• "Manual-first Philosophy: Preference for manual processes over automation, possibly due
to complexity and control requirements"
• "MB5T used for stock in transit checks, crucial for multi-location inventory management"
Key Comment: "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are
automatically integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or
spreadsheet are manually entered by distribution center staff" and "Only customer receiving
automated order acknowledgements (Reliance)"
Unique Aspect: Decentralized DC-based order entry model; ONLY Reliance has EDI and
automated acknowledgement; Manual-first philosophy; OpenText for select modern
trade EDI; Export orders processed in monthly cycles; MB5T for stock in transit checks
(multi-location); Bheem DMS for retail claims; 100% credit block by default; FSSAI license
validation at order creation; TDP days (10/20/30) affect transport capacity
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Decentralized DC-based entry model unique to India;
OpenText integration for Reliance EDI; Manual entry by DC staff workflows; Minimal automation
- high manual dependency; MB5T stock in transit functionality; FSSAI license validation must be
maintained; Default credit block process; TDP days operational logic
 
APAC-JKSEAT (Japan, Korea, SE Asia & Taiwan)
System/Tool:
• Japan: SAP VA01/ME21 (Assumption), Fax, Customer websites, EDI, CSV macro
• KSEAT: SAP VA01 (Assumption), CSV files, Macro/EDI watcher, Email
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fax, websites in Japan) / Automated (CSV macro, EDI for both)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order, Proforma invoice (as order confirmation)
Process Note:
• Japan: "Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer websites,
EDI, and CSV files. Fax and website orders are manually entered into SAP , while EDI and CSV
file orders are automatically integrated into SAP"
• KSEAT: "Orders are received via email as either CSV files or manual requests. CSV files are
uploaded to a shared folder, then picked up by EDI or a macro for automatic entry into SAP ,
while manual orders are entered directly into SAP"
• "Traditional Communication Channels: Japan maintains fax communication alongside
modern channels, reflecting local business culture and customer preferences"
McCain    10|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Advanced Order Planning: Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance eliminates need
for real-time inventory visibility but requires sophisticated planning"
• "CSV Automation: KSEAT uses shared folder mechanism with macro/EDI pickup for CSV
automation"
• "Proforma Invoice as confirmation: KC sends Proforma Invoice (manual PDF from SAP) as
order confirmation; HAVI/McDonald's excluded"
• "No local ATP/MOQ gating at JKC; orders are taken weeks to months ahead (min ~6 weeks),
and sourcing regions (e.g., NA, EU) run ATP on their side"
Key Comment: "Japan: Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer
websites, EDI, and CSV files" and "Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance" and "No local
ATP/MOQ gating at JKC"
Unique Aspect: Traditional fax still used in Japan; CSV macro/shared folder automation
in KSEAT; Proforma invoice used as order confirmation (not standard acknowledgement);
No local ATP - sourcing regions handle ATP; Long lead times (6 weeks to 4 months); ME21 for
purchase orders alongside VA01; Power BI for shipment tracking; Regional orders 6-16 weeks
ahead of ship date
SAP Migration Consideration: Japan VA01/ME21 usage must be validated; Fax integration/
workflow documentation needed; CSV macro automation from shared folders; Proforma invoice
generation process; No local ATP - sourcing region dependency; Long planning horizon (6
weeks-4 months) must be supported; Power BI integration for tracking
 
GREAT BRITAIN (GB)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption for order creation), EDI, Esker, Email (Manual), ZV05N
(for display)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (email orders)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: Manually entered order (from email) / Automated order from EDI/Esker
Process Note:
• "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email (manual entry
required), and Esker (PDF document processing)"
• "Transaction codes used: VA01 for order creation, ZV05N for displaying orders, VA02 for
order amendments"
• "EDI orders undergo only basic sense checks; no comprehensive price validation at entry"
• "if there is an error in the EDI it will generate a failed idoc which can be viewed in WE05"
• "Incompletion log in SAP flags missing data, causing EDI orders to fail and go on hold,
equally if the PO is a duplicate SAP puts the order on hold"
• "Manual orders similarly have no price check at entry; incompletion log identifies gaps"
• "Manual process only - no automatic customer notification upon order entry"
• "All orders entered using t-code VA01, where the order type is specified"
Key Comment: "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email
(manual entry required), and Esker (PDF document processing)" and "Manual process only - no
automatic customer notification upon order entry" and "All orders entered using t-code VA01,
where the order type is specified"
Unique Aspect: ZV05N used for displaying orders (alongside VA03); WE05 for failed IDoc
monitoring; Esker reads PDF documents from customers; No automatic acknowledgement
- manual email confirmation only; No price validation at entry - handled later as claims via
AR inbox; Incompletion log flags missing data; Duplicate PO auto-hold; EDI MDN available on
customer request
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 standard usage confirmed; ZV05N display transaction
validation in S/4HANA; WE05 IDoc monitoring; Esker PDF integration; Manual acknowledgement
McCain    11|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
process; No price validation at entry - pricing claims handled downstream; Incompletion log
dependency
 
EMENA (Europe, Middle East, North Africa)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), ZVA05N (import transaction), EDI, Esker, CSV process
(Italy), Manual (email/phone)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker, CSV Italy) / Manual (email/phone)
Performer: Customer Service Representative / System (for off-time import)
Output: Orders imported into SAP
Process Note:
• "Order requests could be received via various platforms: Manual entry (email & phone call),
ESKER (reads PDF documents from customers), EDI, CSV process (Italy) via EDI"
• "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
• "Only applicable to EMENA"
• T-codes mentioned: "VA01, VA02, ZVA05N, ZG_MTD_SHIPMENT, VL01N, VL02N, VL06"
Key Comment: "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
and "Only applicable to EMENA"
Unique Aspect: ZVA05N import transaction ONLY in EMENA; Off-time import process
unique to this region; CSV process specific to Italy via EDI; Esker PDF document processing;
Multi-channel intake (EDI, Esker, CSV, Manual); Import batch processing during off-peak hours
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ZVA05N off-time import transaction unique to
EMENA must be evaluated for S/4HANA equivalent; Italy CSV process via EDI; Esker integration;
Batch import scheduling and timing; Multi-channel consolidation during off-time import
 
SOUTH AFRICA (SA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Email (Direct), EDI via Vector (Indirect 3PL)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Direct Network) / Automated (Indirect Network via Vector EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Representative (Direct) / Vector System (Indirect)
Output: Manually entered order (Direct) / EDI order via Vector (Indirect)
Process Note:
• "Purchase requests received via email" (Direct Network)
• "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network"
• "Indirect Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from
customers; next day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP
(boundary system), operating on a consignment model"
• "(Direct) Customer Service receives purchase requests via email or manual channels and
manually enters into SAP . T-code VA01 used for order creation"
• "(Indirect) EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers"
• "Proforma invoice/manual order confirmation for some customers"
Key Comment: "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network" and "Indirect
Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers; next
day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP (boundary system),
operating on a consignment model"
Unique Aspect: Dual network model - Direct vs Indirect (3PL/Vector); Vector 3PL ONLY in
South Africa; Consignment model for indirect network; Next-day backward integration to
SAP; Proforma invoice for some customers; T-code VA01 confirmed for order creation; Manual
entry for direct network
McCain    12|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual network model (Direct manual vs Indirect
Vector EDI); Vector 3PL boundary system integration critical; Consignment model logic; Next-day
backward integration timing and process; Proforma invoice generation for specific customers;
VA01 standard usage
(as-is) IT System: SAP , Digital Hub, Excel(CSV), Thalamus
IT Systems:
- SAP ECC
SAP/SSC/Executable: SAP - VA01, VA02
Organization units:  01.05.01.03 Enter Order Into System - Customer Solutions/3PL
Dictionary entries: SAP ECC
Task type: Receive
Enter Consignment Details into the ERP
Task
NORTH AMERICA (NA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Esker, CSV (OpenText), EDI
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (exceptions, new SKUs, data sync
failures)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order with order number generated in SAP
Process Note:
• "EDI & Esker are dominant for order intake (Canada & US domestic orders)"
• "OpenText is heavily used for automated entry (Export orders -- JKSEAT region)"
• "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data sync
failures"
• "For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/ship to and
different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with a full
container load"
• "CSV file transfers are unique to NA, used by Canada domestic for some samples and by
JKSEAT region for customer orders"
Key Comment: "Manual entry and intervention are required for exceptions, new SKUs, or data
sync failures. For export manual changes are required on specific scenarios ex. Same sold to/
ship to and different incoterms depending on loading WH, when samples are loading along with
a full container load."
Unique Aspect: CSV file processing unique to North America; OpenText boundary system
for export orders; Frequent manual review due to diversity of intake channels and customer
changes
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 usage already established; Need to ensure Esker and
OpenText integration validated in S/4HANA; CSV file handling process must be maintained
 
MEXICO
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service, QSR, Retail), SAP VA01 (Assumption), Edicom/EDI
(Retail), Gmail (Food service)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service via Thalamus) / Automated (EDI for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System order generated by platform (Thalamus/EDI/SAP)
Process Note:
McCain    13|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Thalamus is the main platform for food service and QSR; Edicom EDI is used for retail"
• "Manual entry is common, especially for food service; EDI is automated for retail"
• "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer communication"
• "Order acknowledgment can be automated or manual, and may be requested by the
customer"
• "Common check points: price, route, warehouse, weight, duplicate order, requested delivery
date, etc."
Key Comment: "Heavy reliance on Gmail for food service notifications and customer
communication"
Unique Aspect: Gmail extensively used for food service operational communications;
Thalamus serves multiple channels (Food service, QSR, Retail); Edicom EDI specific to retail
channel
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus and Edicom EDI integrations must be validated;
Gmail-based workflow formalization needed; VA01 standard transaction confirmed
 
CALATIN (Central America & Caribbean)
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI (Retail), Day & Ross (DRTN
for NA-sourced)
Manual/Automated: Manual (frequent) / Automated (EDI for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record in SAP
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; SAP for system entry"
• "Manual entry is frequent, especially for exports and smaller segments"
• "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks"
• "NA-sourced orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with full traceability for export
compliance"
Key Comment: "Exports require extra documentation and compliance checks" and "NA-sourced
orders may integrate with Day & Ross (DRTN)"
Unique Aspect: Export-focused operations with enhanced documentation requirements; Day
& Ross (DRTN) integration for NA-sourced orders; Thalamus for food service segment
SAP Migration Consideration: Export documentation workflows must be preserved; DRTN
integration for NA-sourced orders needs validation; Manual export handling processes require
clear procedures in S/4HANA
 
ANDEAN (Colombia)
System/Tool: Thalamus, Carvajal, SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI
Manual/Automated: Manual (portal handling) / Automated (EDI/Carvajal for retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "Thalamus and Carvajal are primary intake platforms; SAP is used for system entry"
• "Manual portal handling, especially for food service (Actualmente se esta migrando a
Thalamus)"
• "EDI/Carvajal standards apply for retail and exports"
McCain    14|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Corrections and change requests are handled manually, with data retention for
compliance"
Key Comment: "Manual portal handling, especially for food service.(Actualmente se esta
migrando a Thalamus)"
Unique Aspect: Carvajal platform specific to Andean region for retail; Migration to Thalamus
in progress; EDI/Carvajal standards for retail and exports
SAP Migration Consideration: Thalamus migration ongoing - ensure smooth transition;
Carvajal integration must be validated in S/4HANA; Data retention for compliance mandatory
 
BRAZIL
System/Tool: SAP Fiori (Primary), SAP ECC VA01 (Exceptions: CPU, McD, BK, maritime,
donations/samples), EDI, Neo Grid (Retail)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fiori entries) / Automated (EDI, Neo Grid)
Performer: CSR (Fiori) / SALES ADM Team (SAP ECC exceptions)
Output: SAP order generated in Fiori or ECC depending on scenario
Process Note:
• "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used for special
cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)"
• "SALES ADM team manages SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
• "WhatsApp/email are common for customer communication and error resolution"
• "Proforma invoices for CPU orders are issued via SAP ECC VF01/VF02"
• "Strong SAP Fiori adoption; exceptions handled manually in ECC"
Key Comment: "SAP Fiori is the main portal for domestic and CPU orders; SAP ECC VA01 is used
for special cases (CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples)" and "SALES ADM team manages
SAP ECC entries for exceptions and special orders"
Unique Aspect: Dual system architecture (Fiori primary + ECC exceptions); SALES ADM team
handles special scenarios; Strong Fiori adoption among all LATAM regions; WhatsApp business
communication; Proforma invoice generation (VF01/VF02)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual system (Fiori + ECC) needs consolidation
strategy in S/4HANA; Special handling for CPU, McD, BK, maritime, donations/samples must be
accommodated; SALES ADM team workflows require clear definition; Proforma invoice process
(VF01/VF02) validation
 
URUGUAY
System/Tool: Thalamus, Power Street, EDI, SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (domestic via Thalamus/Power Street) / Automated (EDI retail)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: System sales order record
Process Note:
• "EDI, Thalamus, and Power Street are used for order intake; Power Street is unique to
Uruguay"
• "Manual entry is common for domestic orders; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and email are standard for customer communication and error resolution"
• "Power Street: Tool to input customer orders for direct and indirect sales. This is used to
load orders on every truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest
of McCain. Orders are loaded on the spot (trucks)."
McCain    15|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Key Comment: "Power Street is unique to Uruguay" and "This is used to load orders on every
truck in Uruguay. In this country, sales is a little different from the rest of McCain. Orders are
loaded on the spot (trucks)."
Unique Aspect: Power Street ONLY exists in Uruguay; On-the-spot truck loading model;
Unique sales operational model different from rest of McCain; Direct and indirect sales via
mobile tool
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Power Street unique to Uruguay must be integrated
or replaced in S/4HANA; Mobile truck loading workflow is business-critical; Operational model
significantly different from other regions
 
ARGENTINA
System/Tool: Thalamus (Food service), Krikos360/EDI (Retail), Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone (QSR),
SAP VA01 (Assumption)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Food service, QSR) / Automated (EDI retail via Krikos360)
Performer: CSR / Operations (varies by channel)
Output: System order by platform (Thalamus/Krikos360/SAP)
Process Note:
• "Thalamus is used for food service; Krikos360/EDI for retail; Gmail/WhatsApp/Phone for
QSR"
• "Manual entry is standard for food service and QSR; EDI for retail"
• "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication"
• "Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy; EDI for retail"
Key Comment: "WhatsApp and Gmail are common for rapid customer communication" and
"Extensive manual review for retail/hardcopy"
Unique Aspect: Krikos360 platform (Planexware) specific to Argentina retail; Multi-channel
digital communication (Gmail, WhatsApp, Phone) for QSR; Channel-specific tool strategy
SAP Migration Consideration: Krikos360/Planexware integration validation critical; WhatsApp/
Gmail workflow formalization needed; Multi-channel tool landscape requires consolidation
assessment
 
APAC-ANZ (Australia & New Zealand)
System/Tool: Portalink, EDI, Email, SAP VA02/VA03 (NOT VA01 for EDI)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Portalink) / Manual (email-based changes)
Performer: Customer Solutions Team
Output: SAP sales order
Process Note:
• "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders, regardless of
how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on email"
• "Portalink Integration: Serves as critical boundary system converting non-EDI orders into
SAP-compatible format, enabling immediate automated acknowledgements for non-EDI
customers"
• "ANZ: VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
• "HighRadius Financial Integration: Used for credit checks and payment tracking"
• "Excel Macro Automation: End-of-day order confirmations automated through Excel
macros"
• "Z7 CSR Hold Implementation: Universal application of Z7 CSR hold enables manual review
and allocation rule enforcement by commercial team"
McCain    16|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Key Comment: "EDI orders are handled automatically in SAP . All changes to customer orders,
regardless of how the original order was received, are processed manually in SAP based on
email" and "VA01 is not used for EDI order entry. VA02 and VA03 is used"
Unique Aspect: CRITICAL: VA01 NOT used for EDI order entry - ANZ uses VA02/VA03;
Portalink boundary system converts non-EDI to SAP format; HighRadius for credit/payment;
Excel macro for end-of-day confirmations; Z7 CSR Hold universal; Duplicate EDI order manual
deletion required
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ANZ does NOT use VA01 for EDI orders (uses VA02/
VA03) - This is UNIQUE and must be carefully evaluated in S/4HANA migration; Portalink
boundary system integration critical; HighRadius integration validation; Excel macro process
may need modernization; Z7 CSR Hold implementation; ATP functionality gap (currently no ATP)
 
APAC-CHINA
System/Tool: Digital Hub (Cloud-based portal - MANDATORY for ALL orders), SAP VA01 (via
EDI integration from Digital Hub)
Manual/Automated: Manual entry into Digital Hub / Automated (Digital Hub to SAP via EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order triggered via EDI from Digital Hub after validation
Process Note:
• "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and processed
through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal"
• "Orders are manually entered into the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are
performed before triggering sales orders into SAP via EDI integration"
• "No Direct Customer EDI: All orders flow through Digital Hub regardless of customer
technical capability"
• "Real-time Integration: Direct integration with warehouse management systems provides
accurate, current inventory data for ATP decisions"
• "Auto-cancellation Logic: Sophisticated cancellation workflows with reason codes (Y1, Y5) for
unpaid orders enable automated revenue protection"
• "ZF delivery blocks preferred over credit blocks for payment terms enforcement"
Key Comment: "All customer purchase orders (retail or food service channels) are received and
processed through the cloud-based Digital Hub portal" and "Orders are manually entered into
the Digital Hub, where validations and changes are performed before triggering sales orders
into SAP via EDI integration"
Unique Aspect: Digital Hub MANDATORY for 100% of orders regardless of channel; No
direct customer-to-SAP EDI; Dual ATP (Digital Hub "light" + SAP "final"); ZF delivery block for
prepayment instead of credit blocks; Auto-cancel Y5 rejection code for unpaid prepay orders;
WMS real-time integration; Channel-specific CRM (Food Service) vs email (Retail/QSR)
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Digital Hub is mandatory gateway (no direct SAP
access); Digital Hub to S/4HANA EDI integration must be validated; Dual ATP mechanism must
be maintained; ZF delivery block logic; Auto-cancellation workflows (Y1, Y5); WMS integration
validation; Prepayment enforcement logic
 
APAC-INDIA
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), EDI via OpenText (Reliance ONLY), Email, Spreadsheet,
Bheem DMS
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI for select modern trade) / Manual (DC staff entry for non-
EDI)
Performer: DC Executives / Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order
McCain    17|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
Process Note:
• "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are automatically
integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or spreadsheet are
manually entered by distribution center staff"
• "DC-based Processing: Decentralized order entry model contrasts with other regions'
centralized approaches"
• "Minimal Automation: Limited automated order acknowledgements (only Reliance) reflects
low digitalization maturity"
• "Reliance Special Treatment: Only customer receiving automated order acknowledgements,
indicating strategic partnership requirements"
• "Manual-first Philosophy: Preference for manual processes over automation, possibly due
to complexity and control requirements"
• "MB5T used for stock in transit checks, crucial for multi-location inventory management"
Key Comment: "EDI orders from select customers in modern trade (e.g.,: Reliance) are
automatically integrated into SAP via OpenText, while non-EDI orders received by email or
spreadsheet are manually entered by distribution center staff" and "Only customer receiving
automated order acknowledgements (Reliance)"
Unique Aspect: Decentralized DC-based order entry model; ONLY Reliance has EDI and
automated acknowledgement; Manual-first philosophy; OpenText for select modern
trade EDI; Export orders processed in monthly cycles; MB5T for stock in transit checks
(multi-location); Bheem DMS for retail claims; 100% credit block by default; FSSAI license
validation at order creation; TDP days (10/20/30) affect transport capacity
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Decentralized DC-based entry model unique to India;
OpenText integration for Reliance EDI; Manual entry by DC staff workflows; Minimal automation
- high manual dependency; MB5T stock in transit functionality; FSSAI license validation must be
maintained; Default credit block process; TDP days operational logic
 
APAC-JKSEAT (Japan, Korea, SE Asia & Taiwan)
System/Tool:
• Japan: SAP VA01/ME21 (Assumption), Fax, Customer websites, EDI, CSV macro
• KSEAT: SAP VA01 (Assumption), CSV files, Macro/EDI watcher, Email
Manual/Automated: Manual (Fax, websites in Japan) / Automated (CSV macro, EDI for both)
Performer: Customer Service Team
Output: SAP sales order, Proforma invoice (as order confirmation)
Process Note:
• Japan: "Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer websites,
EDI, and CSV files. Fax and website orders are manually entered into SAP , while EDI and CSV
file orders are automatically integrated into SAP"
• KSEAT: "Orders are received via email as either CSV files or manual requests. CSV files are
uploaded to a shared folder, then picked up by EDI or a macro for automatic entry into SAP ,
while manual orders are entered directly into SAP"
• "Traditional Communication Channels: Japan maintains fax communication alongside
modern channels, reflecting local business culture and customer preferences"
• "Advanced Order Planning: Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance eliminates need
for real-time inventory visibility but requires sophisticated planning"
• "CSV Automation: KSEAT uses shared folder mechanism with macro/EDI pickup for CSV
automation"
McCain    18|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
• "Proforma Invoice as confirmation: KC sends Proforma Invoice (manual PDF from SAP) as
order confirmation; HAVI/McDonald's excluded"
• "No local ATP/MOQ gating at JKC; orders are taken weeks to months ahead (min ~6 weeks),
and sourcing regions (e.g., NA, EU) run ATP on their side"
Key Comment: "Japan: Customer orders are captured through multiple channels: fax, customer
websites, EDI, and CSV files" and "Orders placed 6 weeks to 4 months in advance" and "No local
ATP/MOQ gating at JKC"
Unique Aspect: Traditional fax still used in Japan; CSV macro/shared folder automation
in KSEAT; Proforma invoice used as order confirmation (not standard acknowledgement);
No local ATP - sourcing regions handle ATP; Long lead times (6 weeks to 4 months); ME21 for
purchase orders alongside VA01; Power BI for shipment tracking; Regional orders 6-16 weeks
ahead of ship date
SAP Migration Consideration: Japan VA01/ME21 usage must be validated; Fax integration/
workflow documentation needed; CSV macro automation from shared folders; Proforma invoice
generation process; No local ATP - sourcing region dependency; Long planning horizon (6
weeks-4 months) must be supported; Power BI integration for tracking
 
GREAT BRITAIN (GB)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption for order creation), EDI, Esker, Email (Manual), ZV05N
(for display)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker) / Manual (email orders)
Performer: Customer Service Representative
Output: Manually entered order (from email) / Automated order from EDI/Esker
Process Note:
• "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email (manual entry
required), and Esker (PDF document processing)"
• "Transaction codes used: VA01 for order creation, ZV05N for displaying orders, VA02 for
order amendments"
• "EDI orders undergo only basic sense checks; no comprehensive price validation at entry"
• "if there is an error in the EDI it will generate a failed idoc which can be viewed in WE05"
• "Incompletion log in SAP flags missing data, causing EDI orders to fail and go on hold,
equally if the PO is a duplicate SAP puts the order on hold"
• "Manual orders similarly have no price check at entry; incompletion log identifies gaps"
• "Manual process only - no automatic customer notification upon order entry"
• "All orders entered using t-code VA01, where the order type is specified"
Key Comment: "GB receives orders through three primary channels: EDI (automated), email
(manual entry required), and Esker (PDF document processing)" and "Manual process only - no
automatic customer notification upon order entry" and "All orders entered using t-code VA01,
where the order type is specified"
Unique Aspect: ZV05N used for displaying orders (alongside VA03); WE05 for failed IDoc
monitoring; Esker reads PDF documents from customers; No automatic acknowledgement
- manual email confirmation only; No price validation at entry - handled later as claims via
AR inbox; Incompletion log flags missing data; Duplicate PO auto-hold; EDI MDN available on
customer request
SAP Migration Consideration: VA01 standard usage confirmed; ZV05N display transaction
validation in S/4HANA; WE05 IDoc monitoring; Esker PDF integration; Manual acknowledgement
process; No price validation at entry - pricing claims handled downstream; Incompletion log
dependency
 
McCain    19|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System  
EMENA (Europe, Middle East, North Africa)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), ZVA05N (import transaction), EDI, Esker, CSV process
(Italy), Manual (email/phone)
Manual/Automated: Automated (EDI, Esker, CSV Italy) / Manual (email/phone)
Performer: Customer Service Representative / System (for off-time import)
Output: Orders imported into SAP
Process Note:
• "Order requests could be received via various platforms: Manual entry (email & phone call),
ESKER (reads PDF documents from customers), EDI, CSV process (Italy) via EDI"
• "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
• "Only applicable to EMENA"
• T-codes mentioned: "VA01, VA02, ZVA05N, ZG_MTD_SHIPMENT, VL01N, VL02N, VL06"
Key Comment: "Orders from various channels are imported into SAP system during off time"
and "Only applicable to EMENA"
Unique Aspect: ZVA05N import transaction ONLY in EMENA; Off-time import process
unique to this region; CSV process specific to Italy via EDI; Esker PDF document processing;
Multi-channel intake (EDI, Esker, CSV, Manual); Import batch processing during off-peak hours
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - ZVA05N off-time import transaction unique to
EMENA must be evaluated for S/4HANA equivalent; Italy CSV process via EDI; Esker integration;
Batch import scheduling and timing; Multi-channel consolidation during off-time import
 
SOUTH AFRICA (SA)
System/Tool: SAP VA01 (Assumption), Email (Direct), EDI via Vector (Indirect 3PL)
Manual/Automated: Manual (Direct Network) / Automated (Indirect Network via Vector EDI)
Performer: Customer Service Representative (Direct) / Vector System (Indirect)
Output: Manually entered order (Direct) / EDI order via Vector (Indirect)
Process Note:
• "Purchase requests received via email" (Direct Network)
• "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network"
• "Indirect Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from
customers; next day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP
(boundary system), operating on a consignment model"
• "(Direct) Customer Service receives purchase requests via email or manual channels and
manually enters into SAP . T-code VA01 used for order creation"
• "(Indirect) EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers"
• "Proforma invoice/manual order confirmation for some customers"
Key Comment: "Direct Network: Orders received manually by delivery network" and "Indirect
Network (3PLs/Vector): EDI captures orders and sends to Vector directly from customers; next
day, orders communicated to McCain for backward integration into SAP (boundary system),
operating on a consignment model"
Unique Aspect: Dual network model - Direct vs Indirect (3PL/Vector); Vector 3PL ONLY in
South Africa; Consignment model for indirect network; Next-day backward integration to
SAP; Proforma invoice for some customers; T-code VA01 confirmed for order creation; Manual
entry for direct network
SAP Migration Consideration: CRITICAL - Dual network model (Direct manual vs Indirect
Vector EDI); Vector 3PL boundary system integration critical; Consignment model logic; Next-day
backward integration timing and process; Proforma invoice generation for specific customers;
VA01 standard usage
McCain    20|Signavio GmbH
L4 - Enter Order Into System

SAP ECC
SAP ECC (ERP Central Component) is the on-premise enterprise resource planning (ERP) system
that integrates digital information created in one area of a business with data from other areas of
the same business in real time.
Application Fact Sheet "SAP ECC" in LeanIX - https://mccain.leanix.net/McCain/factsheet/
Application/0943858b-9833-47b5-af28-f613304cfa87
IT System Alias: SAP ERP
It System Vendor: SAP
Category : Business Application Platform
SAP ECC (Enterprise Central Component)
SAP ECC (ERP Central Component) is a comprehensive enterprise resource planning (ERP)
software suite developed by SAP . It serves as the core component of many organizations’ IT
landscapes, integrating key business processes and data across departments, and enabling
efficient management of resources, operations, and reporting.Key 
Features of SAP ECC:Centralized database for seamless data flow across business functions
• Modular structure, allowing organizations to implement only the modules they need
• Real-time processing and reporting capabilities
• Strong integration with other SAP and third-party systems
SAP CO, a module within SAP ECC that focuses on internal accounting and management
accounting, providing tools for planning, controlling, and analyzing costs and revenues. It is tightly
integrated with SAP FI (Financial Accounting) and is often referred to as SAP FICO. SAP CO helps
businesses track costs associated with various business processes, allocate costs, and manage
profitability analysis. 
IT System Alias: SAP ECC
IT System Provider: SAP
IT System Type: Application
